QF.id: XIII Introduction to the Underlying Markets_22

question.text: In the equity market, what is the chronological order of derivative product introduction in India?

answer.text: Index Futures (June 2000) → Index Options (June 2001) → Stock Options (July 2001) → Stock Futures (November 2001)
Stock Futures (June 2000) → Stock Options (June 2001) → Index Futures (July 2001) → Index Options (November 2001)
Index Options (June 2000) → Index Futures (June 2001) → Stock Futures (July 2001) → Stock Options (November 2001)
Stock Options (June 2000) → Stock Futures (June 2001) → Index Options (July 2001) → Index Futures (November 2001)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI permitted equity derivatives starting with index futures on NIFTY/SENSEX in June 2000. Index options followed in June 2001, stock options in July 2001, and stock futures in November 2001. The progression was from less complex to more complex products. **The Trap:** Many candidates assume stock-level products came before index-level, but regulators introduced index derivatives first as they carry less manipulation risk. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII Ch1 covers this chronology. Series I notes currency futures started in 2008. Series IV notes IR futures introduction. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Regulators globally introduce index derivatives before single-stock derivatives — this pattern is consistent across equity, currency, and commodity markets.
